title: Positron Emission Tomography (PET)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical medical-imaging literature for this principle.

E.description: Positron Emission Tomography (PET): pet coincidence produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.annihilation_511keV -> D.coincidence_detector -> L.backproject -> int.angular, with multi-angle tomographic integration and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D activity concentration. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~14; calibration-level mismatch (random_coincidences, scatter, attenuation_correction_error) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: n_projections(u, v) = integral A(r) * attenuation(r) along LOR + randoms + scatter + n
E.world_state_x: Patient-side quantity of interest (attenuation / activity / perfusion / stiffness etc.)
E.observation_y: Clinical sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D activity concentration is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 14); random_coincidences dominates the stability cliff; scatter and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [FBP-PET] | iterative projection (ADMM / GAP) + optimisation [OSEM] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [DeepPET]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |random_coincidences| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
